title: angraecum meirax
intro: Angraecum meirax, also known as the 'Congo orchid', is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to the tropical forests of central Africa. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Angraecum meirax are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat wounds, making it a valuable herbal remedy in its native region. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and crushed to create a powder, which can be mixed with water to create a tea or infused in oil for topical applications.
